frameworks.name: Hebbian / NMDA-dependent synaptic plasticity
frameworks.one_line: Structural spine plasticity follows NMDA-receptor-gated activity-dependent rules

frameworks.name: structure-follows-function (activity-dependent rewiring)
frameworks.one_line: Functional plasticity from DCS extended to dendritic-spine structural correlate

core_question: Does anodal direct-current stimulation paired with motor training drive NMDA-receptor-dependent structural synaptic plasticity in sensorimotor cortex?
key_assumptions: Dendritic spine formation indexes structural synaptic plasticity
NMDA receptors gate activity-dependent plasticity in M1 pyramidal neurons
Anodal DCS produces functional plasticity that can have a structural correlate
manipulations: Anodal DCS alone vs motor training alone vs paired DCS+training
key_findings: Paired anodal DCS + motor training increased spine formation in M1 layer 2/3 pyramidal neurons beyond either intervention alone
Spine formation gain was abolished in NMDA-receptor knockouts
interpretations: DCS-paired motor learning induces structural plasticity that follows the same NMDA-dependent rules as DCS-induced functional plasticity
